puzzle.title: The Burden of Riches
puzzle.intro: You stand before a tale of immense fortune, a life adorned with gold and power, yet ultimately a cautionary lesson for all who seek worldly grandeur. Can you unravel the identity of this figure whose story echoes through time, a testament to the fleeting nature of worldly possessions?
clues: I was from the people of a great Prophet, but I chose a path of arrogance and oppression.
Allah bestowed upon me immense wealth, so much that the keys to my treasures were a heavy burden for a strong group of men.
My people advised me not to exult in my riches, reminding me that Allah does not love the exultant.
I arrogantly claimed that my vast fortune was due to my own knowledge and effort, not a blessing from Allah.
I was warned against corruption and reminded to seek the Hereafter with what Allah had given me, but I refused to listen and continued in my pride.
Eventually, Allah caused the earth to swallow me and my dwelling, leaving me with no helpers against His decree.
puzzle.verse: So We caused the earth to swallow him and his home. And there was for him no body of supporters to aid him other than Allah, nor was he of those who could defend themselves. (Al-Qasas 28:81)
puzzle.arabic: فَخَسَفْنَا بِهِ وَبِدَارِهِ الْأَرْضَ فَمَا كَانَ لَهُ مِن فِئَةٍ يَنصُرُونَهُ مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ وَمَا كَانَ مِنَ الْمُنتَصِرِينَ
